backlog.title: Region TUI Input-Surface Bugfixes (Round 2)
backlog.description: Fix the three region-TUI gaps from end-to-end creative validation round 2: (1) wire up mouse support (PRD §7.6) — currently decoded but discarded; (2) stop Ctrl-z/SIGTSTP from freezing the popup with the terminal unrestored; (3) make Ctrl-c exit 1 per §7.5 (currently 130). Issues 2 and 3 share a single one-line root cause (ISIG not disabled in app.makeRaw). All work is scoped to the region TUI input surface; the core capture→HTML flows and keyboard selection are unaffected. See plan/004_92f7aeb62a3a/bugfix/002_b76ad57f9c71/architecture/ for validated findings.

milestones.title: TTY control-key / signal robustness (Issues 2 & 3 — ISIG root cause)
milestones.description: Disable ISIG in app.makeRaw so tty control characters (Ctrl-c/Ctrl-z/Ctrl-\) arrive as inert bytes that input.zig classifies, instead of being translated into signals by the kernel. This one-line fix resolves BOTH Issue 2 (Ctrl-z no longer suspends/freezes) and Issue 3 (Ctrl-c ⇒ exit 1 via the existing 0x03→.quit mapping, not 130). palette.zig's separate raw-mode is out of scope. Add a regression harness that drives the real binary through a pty with 0x1a/0x03.

tasks.title: Disable ISIG in app.makeRaw + unit test (root-cause fix for Issues 2 & 3)
tasks.description: Add `raw.lflag.ISIG = false;` to app.makeRaw (src/tui/app.zig) and update the makeRaw unit test to prove the flag is cleared. This is the one-line root-cause fix the PRD recommends; it makes input.zig's existing 0x03→.quit mapping live (Ctrl-c ⇒ exit 1) and makes 0x1a an inert swallowed byte (Ctrl-z ⇒ no suspend).

subtasks.title: Add `raw.lflag.ISIG = false;` to makeRaw; update makeRaw unit tests + doc-comment
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: app.makeRaw (src/tui/app.zig:94) clears ICANON/ECHO (lflag), IXON/ICRNL/BRKINT (iflag), OPOST (oflag); sets CS8; MIN=1/TIME=0 — but NOT ISIG. installSignalHandlers (app.zig) installs SIGINT/SIGTERM/SIGQUIT only (no SIGTSTP). With ISIG on, Ctrl-c→SIGINT→restore+reraise→exit 130 (Issue 3); Ctrl-z→SIGTSTP→stopped, terminal unrestored (Issue 2). input.decodeByte ALREADY maps 0x03⇒.quit (dead while ISIG on). See architecture/isig_fix_design.md + architecture/system_context.md.
2. INPUT: the existing PURE `pub fn makeRaw(original: std.posix.termios) std.posix.termios` (src/tui/app.zig ~line 94) and its two unit tests ("makeRaw: clears ICANON/ECHO/IXON/ICRNL/BRKINT/OPOST …" and "makeRaw: idempotent over an already-raw input …").
3. LOGIC: (a) add `raw.lflag.ISIG = false;` in makeRaw immediately after the `raw.lflag.ECHO = false;` line. (b) Update makeRaw's doc-comment (~line 90) to list ISIG among the cleared lflag flags and note WHY (so Ctrl-c/z/\ arrive as bytes input.zig classifies, not kernel signals). (c) In the first makeRaw test, ADD `input.lflag.ISIG = true;` to the input termios (it is false in a zeroed termios, so the assertion must prove makeRaw clears a SET flag) and ADD `try std.testing.expectEqual(false, raw.lflag.ISIG);` to the assertions. (d) In the idempotent test, assert ISIG stays false. Do NOT touch palette.zig (it has its OWN separate raw-mode for the short sync-palette OSC query — out of scope). Do NOT modify input.zig/motion.zig/select.zig/view.zig/region.zig. Verify `zig build test -Doptimize=ReleaseFast` stays green (275 fns — the change is additive; no test asserts ISIG==true). Mock nothing (pure termios value tests).
4. OUTPUT: app.makeRaw now produces an ISIG=false termios; the region TUI's Ctrl-c/Ctrl-z/Ctrl-\ arrive as readable bytes. Consumed by P1.M1.T1.S2 (the harness asserts the new behavior) and unblocks the canonical cancel path used elsewhere.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] update the makeRaw doc-comment (src/tui/app.zig) to document ISIG among cleared flags + the rationale. No public API / config / env / CLI surface change — no README/CONFIGURATION.md edit here (that is the final P1.M3 docs task).

tasks.title: Regression harness for Ctrl-z (no suspend) + Ctrl-c (exit 1)
tasks.description: Add tests/region_signal_keys.sh — a python3 pty drive over an isolated tmux socket that sends 0x1a (Ctrl-z) and 0x03 (Ctrl-c) to the real region binary and asserts Ctrl-z does NOT suspend the process (status line keeps repainting) and Ctrl-c exits 1 (not 130) with no output/sidecar. Follows the canonical tests/region_empty_confirm.sh pattern.

subtasks.title: Create tests/region_signal_keys.sh (python3 pty: 0x1a no-suspend, 0x03 exit-1)
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: canonical harness = tests/region_empty_confirm.sh (set -u; cd repo root; SKIP exit 0 if tmux/python3 absent; REAL_TMUX=$(command -v tmux); build release if missing; SOCK="t2h-<tag>-$$"; WORK=mktemp -d on real disk; a PATH shim `shim/tmux` that `exec "$REAL_TMUX" -L "$SOCK" "$@"` (absolute path, NO recursion, NO append log ⇒ check-safety clean); new-session -d -s s; trap kill-session -t s + rm -rf WORK on EXIT; python3 pty.fork drive). Signal bytes: 0x1a=Ctrl-z, 0x03=Ctrl-c. Use os.waitstatus_to_exitcode(status) (NOT the raw `>>` bit-shift token — it trips check-safety R3). See architecture/testing_safety.md + architecture/isig_fix_design.md.
2. INPUT: the fixed release binary from P1.M1.T1.S1 (ISIG off ⇒ Ctrl-c exits 1, Ctrl-z is an inert byte).
3. LOGIC: create tests/region_signal_keys.sh mirroring region_empty_confirm.sh EXACTLY for the safety shell. Pane: `tmux -L "$SOCK" new-session -d -s s -x 25 -y 6`; seed `echo hi` (sleep 0.4). python3 pty.fork the binary `region --target $PANE`: (a) CTRL-z: write b"\x1a", drain ~0.4s, write b"j", drain ~0.4s, read emitted bytes; ASSERT the process is NOT stopped (os.waitpid(pid, WNOHANG) ⇒ 0 / still readable) AND the status-line `row:` value CHANGED after `j` (TUI kept repainting ⇒ never suspended); then write b"q" and ASSERT a clean exit (status ⇒ exited). (b) CTRL-c: fresh drive; write b"\x03"; `code = os.waitstatus_to_exitcode(status)`; ASSERT code == 1 (NOT 130); ASSERT no --output file exists AND no `.last-output` sidecar references the output. (c) comparison: a `q` drive asserts exit 1. Wrap the python as `PATH="$SHIM:$PATH" python3 - "$PANE" … <<'PYEOF' || fail "…"`. Honor PRD §0/§0.1 fully: isolated socket, teardown by named session ONLY, never kill-server/killall/pkill, scratch on real disk. SKIP cleanly (exit 0) if tmux/python3 absent.
4. OUTPUT: a self-contained PASS/exit-0 harness that FAILS (exit 1) if Ctrl-z suspends the process or Ctrl-c exits != 1. Runnable standalone (`sh tests/region_signal_keys.sh`) and safe in CI.
5. DOCS: none — this is an integration test harness; no user-facing / config / API surface change.

milestones.title: Mouse support wiring (Issue 1 — PRD §7.6)
milestones.description: Make mouse input functional in the region overlay: click moves the cursor; drag selects (linewise by default, block with Alt); wheel scrolls. app.zig already enables SGR mouse reporting and decodes every event into app.MouseEvent, but regionHandle has no .mouse arm so the decoded events are discarded. The fix is pure glue over existing unit-tested primitives (motion.Cursor, select.Sel, view.* scroll fns), implemented entirely in region.zig: a pure coordinate-conversion fn, a pure mouse state-machine fn, the RegionCtx.mouse_anchor field, and a .mouse arm in regionHandle. Plus an integration harness that SGR-clicks/drags/wheels the real binary through a pty.

tasks.title: Pure mouse→grid coordinate + state-machine core (no I/O)
tasks.description: Two pure, unit-testable functions in region.zig: mouseCell (1-based SGR cell → 0-based grid cell, honoring viewport scroll + excluding the status-line row) and applyMouse (the press/motion/release/wheel state machine mutating cursor+sel+mouse_anchor). Plus clampCursorIntoViewport. All operate on stack value structs / explicit pointers — NO Terminal construction ⇒ safe as separate test fns.

subtasks.title: Add pure mouseCell() SGR→grid coordinate conversion + unit tests
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: SGR MouseEvent.x/y are 1-BASED character cells on the popup screen (src/tui/app.zig MouseEvent doc). view.render maps grid row gy = viewport.scroll + vy where vy = (screen row - 1), and grid col gx = (screen col - 1); the status line occupies screen row `tty_rows` = viewport row `grid_rows` (grid area is rows 0..grid_rows-1). So a 1-based click (sx,sy) ⇒ gx=sx-1, vy=sy-1, gy=scroll+vy. See architecture/mouse_wiring_design.md + architecture/system_context.md.
2. INPUT: `app.MouseEvent` (src/tui/app.zig: fields x:u32, y:u32 — 1-based) and `view.Pos` (src/tui/view.zig: {x:u32, y:u32}). Consumed by applyMouse (P1.M2.T1.S2).
3. LOGIC: add a module-private PURE fn in src/region.zig: `fn mouseCell(sx: u32, sy: u32, scroll: u32, grid_rows: u16, total_rows: u32, tty_cols: u16) view.Pos`. Compute gx = clamp(sx-1, 0, tty_cols-1); vy = clamp(sy-1, 0, grid_rows-1) [excludes the status-line row]; gy = clamp(scroll + vy, 0, total_rows-1). Use SATURATING arithmetic (`@as(u32, tty_cols) -| 1`, `@as(u32, grid_rows) -| 1`, `+|`) and guard total_rows==0 so there is NO u32 underflow. Return .{ .x = gx, .y = gy }. Add separate `test` fns (mouseCell is PURE — constructs NO Terminal ⇒ safe as a separate test fn in region.zig, which already has many such pure-helper tests): plain click maps sx-1/sy-1; a click on the status-line row (sy > grid_rows) clamps vy to grid_rows-1 (last grid row); scroll offset is applied (gy = scroll+vy); gx clamps to tty_cols-1; gy clamps to total_rows-1; degenerate grid_rows==0 and tty_cols==0 do NOT underflow. Mock nothing.
4. OUTPUT: the pure mouseCell fn, consumed by applyMouse in P1.M2.T1.S2.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] add a doc-comment on mouseCell documenting the 1-based→0-based conversion, the viewport.scroll offset, and the status-line-row exclusion. No user-facing / config surface change.

subtasks.title: Add pure applyMouse() state machine + clampCursorIntoViewport() + unit tests
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: building blocks (all PURE, unit-tested): motion.Cursor {pos:view.Pos, viewport:view.Viewport} (src/tui/motion.zig); select.Sel {anchor, cursor, mode, pub begin/clear/active/toggle} + select.Mode {.none,.linewise,.block} (src/tui/select.zig); view.scrollForCursor/halfPageUp/halfPageDown (src/tui/view.zig). PRD §7.6 semantics: click moves cursor; drag selects (linewise default, block with Alt); wheel scrolls. tmux copy-mode-mouse parity: a fresh click discards any prior selection; a drag begins at the press cell and extends to the cursor; a plain click (no drag) leaves no selection. See architecture/mouse_wiring_design.md.
2. INPUT: mouseCell from P1.M2.T1.S1; app.MouseEvent (action ∈ press/release/motion/wheel_up/wheel_down; alt/shift/ctrl bools); motion.Cursor; select.Sel; select.Mode; view.scrollForCursor/halfPageUp/halfPageDown.
3. LOGIC: add two module-private PURE fns in src/region.zig taking EXPLICIT pointers (so they are testable WITHOUT a RegionCtx/Screen): `fn applyMouse(cursor:*motion.Cursor, sel:*select.Sel, mouse_anchor:*?view.Pos, m:app.MouseEvent, grid_rows:u16, total_rows:u32, tty_cols:u16) void` and `fn clampCursorIntoViewport(cursor:*motion.Cursor, sel:*select.Sel, total_rows:u32, grid_rows:u16) void`. applyMouse: (press) cursor.pos=cell; cursor.viewport.scroll=scrollForCursor(cell.y,…); sel.clear(); sel.cursor=cursor.pos; mouse_anchor.*=cell. (motion) if mouse_anchor.* set: want_mode = if(m.alt) .block else .linewise; if !sel.active() sel.begin(anchor, want_mode) else if sel.mode!=want_mode sel.mode=want_mode; cursor.pos=cell; scrollForCursor; sel.cursor=cursor.pos. else: just move cursor.pos + scrollForCursor. (release) if mouse_anchor set: cursor.pos=cell; scrollForCursor; sel.cursor=cursor.pos; mouse_anchor.*=null; if sel.active() and anchor==cursor (a click, no drag) sel.clear(). (wheel_up) viewport.scroll=halfPageUp(scroll,total,grid_rows); clampCursorIntoViewport. (wheel_down) halfPageDown; clamp. clampCursorIntoViewport: clamp cursor.pos.y into [scroll, min(total-1, scroll+grid_rows-1)] (saturating); sel.cursor=cursor.pos. `cell` = mouseCell(m.x,m.y,cursor.viewport.scroll,grid_rows,total_rows,tty_cols). Add separate `test` fns with STACK-allocated cursor/sel/mouse_anchor (NO Terminal ⇒ safe): press moves cursor+clears sel+sets anchor; motion-after-press begins linewise at anchor and extends (extent=anchor..cursor); alt-drag ⇒ block (extent rect); toggling alt mid-drag switches mode; release-after-drag keeps the selection; release-of-a-click (no motion) clears the selection; wheel_up/wheel_down move viewport.scroll by half-page and clamp the cursor into view; motion with no prior press just moves the cursor. Mock nothing — all pure value structs.
4. OUTPUT: applyMouse + clampCursorIntoViewport pure fns, consumed by the regionHandle wiring in P1.M2.T2.S1.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] doc-comments on applyMouse + clampCursorIntoViewport documenting the §7.6 state machine (click/drag/wheel, Alt⇒block, half-page wheel). No user-facing / config surface change.

tasks.title: Wire the .mouse arm into regionHandle + add RegionCtx.mouse_anchor
tasks.description: Connect the pure mouse logic to the live event loop: add a `mouse_anchor: ?view.Pos` field to RegionCtx and a `.mouse` arm to regionHandle (before the input.feed call, since feed returns null for mouse) that calls applyMouse + repaint. Update the stale doc-comment that called mouse a NO-OP follow-up.

subtasks.title: Add RegionCtx.mouse_anchor field + .mouse arm in regionHandle + doc-comment update
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: regionHandle (src/region.zig ~line 137) is the SOLE app.EventHandler wired into app.runEvents (region.zig ~line 420). It currently calls input.feed(&ctx.decoder, ev); input.feed returns null for .eof/.mouse (src/tui/input.zig: `.eof, .mouse => return null`), so decoded mouse events fall through to `return .none` and are DROPPED. regionHandle's doc-comment (~line 135) concedes mouse is an unfinished "follow-up". RegionCtx (src/region.zig:88) owns cursor/sel/mgrid/etc. as a stack var. Search-mode (handleSearchByte) already returns .none for non-key events, so mouse is naturally ignored while typing a search pattern. See architecture/mouse_wiring_design.md.
2. INPUT: applyMouse + clampCursorIntoViewport + mouseCell from P1.M2.T1.S1/S2; the existing repaint(ctx) fn (src/region.zig); RegionCtx; app.Event/.MouseEvent.
3. LOGIC: (a) add `mouse_anchor: ?view.Pos = null` as a field on RegionCtx (src/region.zig:88, after `sel`). (b) In regionHandle, immediately AFTER the `if (ctx.searching) return handleSearchByte(ctx, ev);` line and BEFORE the `if (input.feed(...))` call, add: `switch (ev) { .mouse => |m| { applyMouse(&ctx.cursor, &ctx.sel, &ctx.mouse_anchor, m, ctx.grid_rows, ctx.total_rows, ctx.tty_cols); repaint(ctx) catch {}; return .none; }, else => {} }`. (repaint errors are swallowed — matches the existing resilient-write stance.) (c) Update regionHandle's doc-comment: REMOVE the "Mouse is a NO-OP / follow-up" note and the stale "input.feed returns null … on .mouse" framing; state that mouse is now wired (PRD §7.6: click moves cursor, drag selects linewise/block-on-Alt, wheel scrolls), consumed before the keyboard decoder. Do NOT modify input.zig/motion.zig/select.zig/view.zig/app.zig. Verify `zig build test -Doptimize=ReleaseFast` (275 fns) AND all 4 shipped harnesses (tests/*.sh) stay GREEN — the change is a NEW branch; the keyboard path is untouched.
4. OUTPUT: the region TUI now responds to SGR mouse input (click/drag/wheel) through the existing decoded app.MouseEvent stream; regionHandle consumes .mouse instead of discarding it. Consumed by the integration harness P1.M2.T3.S1.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] regionHandle doc-comment updated (removes the stale follow-up note; documents the §7.6 mouse arm). No README/CONFIGURATION.md change here — that is the final P1.M3 docs task.

tasks.title: Mouse integration harness (Issue 1 / §7.6 validation)
tasks.description: Add tests/region_mouse.sh — a python3 pty drive over an isolated tmux socket that sends real SGR mouse sequences (click/drag/wheel) to the region binary and asserts: a click moves the cursor (status row:/col: changes), a drag→confirm writes a correct HTML file, and the wheel scrolls the viewport. Follows the canonical tests/region_empty_confirm.sh pattern.

subtasks.title: Create tests/region_mouse.sh (python3 pty: SGR click/drag/wheel assertions)
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: SGR mode-1006 format `\x1b[<{b};{x};{y}{M|m}` — x,y are 1-BASED cells; M=press/motion/wheel, m=release; b encodes button+mods (0=left, +4 shift, +8 alt, +16 ctrl, +32 motion, 64 wheel-up, 65 wheel-down). Concretely: left press (col=15,row=2) = b"\x1b[<0;15;2M"; left motion/drag (col=15,row=3) = b"\x1b[<32;15;3M"; left release = b"\x1b[<0;15;3m"; wheel up = b"\x1b[<64;1;1M"; wheel down = b"\x1b[<65;1;1M"; alt-drag = b"\x1b[<40;…;…M" (40=32|8). Canonical harness = tests/region_empty_confirm.sh. The region TUI's initial cursor is at the LAST row, col 0; the status line shows `row:N col:M` (1-based). See architecture/testing_safety.md + architecture/mouse_wiring_design.md.
2. INPUT: the mouse-wired release binary from P1.M2.T2.S1 (regionHandle consumes .mouse).
3. LOGIC: create tests/region_mouse.sh mirroring region_empty_confirm.sh EXACTLY for the safety shell (set -u; cd repo; SKIP exit 0 if tmux/python3 absent; REAL_TMUX; build release if missing; SOCK="t2h-mouse-$$"; WORK=mktemp -d on real disk; PATH shim `exec "$REAL_TMUX" -L "$SOCK" "$@"`; new-session -d -s s -x 25 -y 8; seed a few distinct marker lines e.g. "printf 'LNE0XY content\n'" repeated so rows are identifiable; trap kill-session -t s + rm -rf WORK). Drive via python3 pty.fork `region --target $PANE`: (a) CLICK-MOVES-CURSOR: drain ~0.7s, extract the status-line `col:M` from emitted bytes; send a left click at a known FAR cell (e.g. b"\x1b[<0;15;2M"); drain ~0.4s; re-extract col:; ASSERT col: changed toward the clicked column (proves regionHandle consumed the .mouse event). (b) DRAG-CONFIRM-WRITES-FILE: press at A (b"\x1b[<0;2;1M") → motion to B 3+ rows apart (b"\x1b[<32;2;4M") → release (b"\x1b[<0;2;4m") → Enter (b"\r"); ASSERT an --output HTML file exists AND contains a seeded marker line (proves drag-select began + extended + confirm rendered). (c) WHEEL-SCROLLS: capture status `row:`; send wheel_up (b"\x1b[<64;1;1M"); assert the viewport scrolled (the status `row:` shifted / the cursor clamped into the new viewport). Use os.waitstatus_to_exitcode (NOT the `>>` bit-shift token). Wrap as `PATH="$SHIM:$PATH" python3 - … <<'PYEOF' || fail "…"`. Honor PRD §0/§0.1 fully: isolated socket, teardown by named session ONLY, scratch on real disk. SKIP cleanly (exit 0) if tmux/python3 absent.
4. OUTPUT: a self-contained PASS/exit-0 harness that FAILS (exit 1) if mouse input is ignored (the pre-fix state). Runnable standalone and CI-safe.
5. DOCS: none — this is an integration test harness; no user-facing / config / API surface change.

milestones.title: Sync changeset-level documentation
milestones.description: Final cross-cutting docs sweep (Mode B): update README.md's region/copy-mode-TUI feature section and docs/CONFIGURATION.md's region-keys reference to reflect that mouse is now supported (§7.6) and that Ctrl-c exits 1 / Ctrl-z is ignored. No new options/env/flags were added. This task depends on ALL implementing subtasks and runs last.

tasks.title: Sync changeset-level documentation (README + CONFIGURATION)
tasks.description: Update the user-facing docs that summarize the region TUI to surface mouse support and the corrected signal-key behavior. README.md feature blurbs + docs/CONFIGURATION.md region-keys reference.

subtasks.title: Update README.md region/mouse feature section + docs/CONFIGURATION.md region-keys reference
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: README.md has a region / copy-mode-TUI feature section describing the keyboard-driven selection; docs/CONFIGURATION.md documents options + bindings (and likely a region-keys reference). The implemented behavior: mouse is now functional (§7.6 — click moves cursor, drag selects linewise / block with Alt, wheel scrolls); Ctrl-c cancels and exits 1 (§7.5); Ctrl-z is ignored (no suspend). NO new option, env var, or CLI flag was added by this changeset — do not invent any.
2. INPUT: completed implementing subtasks — P1.M1.T1.S1 (ISIG off), P1.M1.T2.S1 (signal-key harness), P1.M2.T2.S1 (mouse wired into regionHandle), P1.M2.T3.S1 (mouse harness).
3. LOGIC: (a) README.md — in the region / copy-mode TUI feature section, add mouse to the input surface description ALONGSIDE the keyboard (click to move cursor; drag to select, linewise by default and block with Alt; wheel to scroll), and ensure the cancel behavior reflects Ctrl-c ⇒ exit 1 (grouped with q/Esc) and Ctrl-z is ignored. Keep it a concise feature blurb, not a keystroke table. (b) docs/CONFIGURATION.md — in any region-keys / keybindings reference, add the mouse bindings + the corrected Ctrl-c exit code (1, not 130) if such a reference exists; if the file only covers tmux options/bindings (not in-TUI keys), make only the minimal accurate edit. Do NOT add configuration knobs that do not exist. Read the current README.md + docs/CONFIGURATION.md region sections first and edit only what the changeset actually changed. Verify the docs build/read cleanly (markdown lint if the repo has one; otherwise eyeball).
4. OUTPUT: README.md + docs/CONFIGURATION.md accurately reflect mouse support (§7.6) and the corrected Ctrl-c/Ctrl-z behavior.
5. DOCS: [Mode B] this IS the changeset-level documentation-sync task; it depends on every implementing subtask and runs LAST so the overview docs summarize a coherent, complete delta.
